title: malaxis xerophila
intro: Malaxis xerophila, also known as the desert green adder's mouth, is a species of orchid found in arid regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Malaxis xerophila include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and protecting against infections. Herbal preparations of Malaxis xerophila can be made by infusing the plant's leaves and roots in water or oil to create a tincture or salve that can be applied topically to the affected area.
